newsSince: No NEW material news since June 9. The ~$1.65B AMI acquisition (cash+stock, $1.15B financing, expected Q3 2026 close) was announced May 4 and is established context. June price action is AI-trade flow/profit-taking, not fundamental. Strong 2Q26 outlook and multiple bank PTs ($140-165) reflect coverage, not new alpha.

sources.label: LSCC Q1 FY26 results / AMI setup
Lattice to acquire AMI in $1.65B deal (May 4)
LSCC down 6% on AMI financing / balance-sheet bet

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: LSCC stays a selective, indirect AI/edge/power node, less direct than HBM/packaging/networking. The AMI deal (May 4) broadens server platform-control content but predates June 9. No new node-relevance evidence; hold.
verdicts.bull: AMI adds platform firmware/manageability content for AI servers; low-power FPGA control plane.
verdicts.neutral: Indirect AI node; recovery + acquisition story largely priced.
verdicts.bear: +85% YTD, integration risk on AMI, high multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: control-plane/edge-inference optionality, not a co-packaged-optics bottleneck. AMI deal is pre-June 9; multiple bank PTs in the $140-165 zone show coverage is no longer under-owned. No fresh CPO catalyst; hold.
verdicts.bull: Secure system-control optionality alongside CPO buildouts.
verdicts.neutral: Not a CPO chokepoint; crowded post-Computex coverage.
verdicts.bear: Indirect to CPO; valuation full; AMI dilution.

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: good company, weak hidden-alpha setup after the run ($20B cap, +85% YTD, edge-AI not humanoid-pure). June 9 already moved it down for crowding/scale. No new robotics-specific evidence since; hold.
verdicts.bull: Edge-AI/power-efficient silicon relevant to robotics compute.
verdicts.bear: Not a direct humanoid play; large cap, crowded.
